svg.title: U-Net architecture for monocular depth estimation
svg.desc: Flowchart showing encoder path going down, bottleneck, decoder path going up, with skip connections across, producing a depth map

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the RGB input image look like for depth estimation?')
sendPrompt('Explain the first encoder block in a U-Net — what are conv layers, BatchNorm, and ReLU?')
g.text: Conv → BN → ReLU → Conv → BN → ReLU

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does max pooling do in a CNN encoder?')
sendPrompt('How does the second encoder block differ from the first in terms of feature map size and channels?')
sendPrompt('What does max pooling do in a CNN encoder?')
sendPrompt('What is the bottleneck in a U-Net and what does it capture?')
sendPrompt('What is transposed convolution and why is it used in the U-Net decoder?')
sendPrompt('How does the first decoder block reconstruct spatial resolution in U-Net?')
sendPrompt('How does the final 1x1 conv produce a depth map from the decoder output?')
